gestures.mdmPrompt: The person stands tall, opens both arms wide, and smiles while introducing subtraction confidently.
A tutor steps slightly forward, raises the right hand to point at an imaginary number of apples while explaining the example.
The tutor leans forward slightly, raises both hands with fingers poised to count, welcoming answers enthusiastically.
A person shifts weight to the left, uses the left hand to emphasize subtracting two as they explain.
The tutor stands tall, spreads both arms wide open, inviting responses and highlighting the final question clearly.
The person waves both hands gently, signaling to regroup and introducing subtraction in a calm, cheerful manner.
The tutor steps forward again, points decisively with the right hand to emphasize the new example about the candies.
The person leans forward eagerly, raising both hands with fingers extended as if inviting the students to count together.
A person shifts to the left slightly, gesturing with the left hand to stress the importance of the subtraction in the question.
The tutor stands confidently, arms crossed then opens both hands wide, reiterating the final problem with clear enthusiasm.
